title: Illinois Accountability Commission: Federal Agents Used 'Illegal and Violent Conduct' in Operation Midway Blitz
excerpt: A state commission created by Illinois Governor JB Pritzker issued its final report finding that federal ICE and CBP agents engaged in unconstitutional uses of force, routine lying, and command-directed misconduct during the 2025 Chicago-area immigration crackdown — and recommending prosecution of frontline agents.
content:
On April 30, 2026, the Illinois Accountability Commission — chaired by former federal chief judge Ruben Castillo — published its final report on Operation Midway Blitz, the Trump administration's intensive immigration enforcement campaign in the Chicago metropolitan area from September through November 2025.

  • The commission found federal agents engaged in "extreme physical force, indiscriminate use of chemical agents, shootings, beatings, and other violent acts," while White House officials routinely lied to conceal the operation's true motivations.
  • Evidence showed agents deployed Blackhawk helicopters, tear gas, and paramilitary tactics; stopped people "based on how they looked, how they spoke or where they worked"; and lied in reports about their vehicles being boxed in or threats of being shot by unarmed people.
  • Commission Vice Chair Patricia Brown Holmes stated: "The evidence led straight up to the White House."
  • The commission referred its findings to Illinois State Police and Cook County prosecutors for investigation and potential criminal prosecution of frontline agents.
The report recommends prohibiting "roving" patrols, ending warrantless arrests, halting paramilitary tactics, and requiring body-worn cameras for all immigration agents.

The commission also found "reasonable cause to believe that federal agents shot and killed Silverio Villegas González without apparent justification" — citing a DHS use-of-force policy the agents violated by shooting to disable a moving vehicle.
